knowledge.html:
Cac Cau Truc Viet Lai Cau

  • Too...to: S + V + too + adj/adv + (for sb) + to do st
  • Enough: S + V + adj/adv + enough + (for sb) + to do st
  • So...that: S + V + so + adj/adv + that + S + V
  • Such...that: It + V + such + (a/an) + N(s) + that + S + V
  • Have/get something done: have/get + st + Ved/V3
  • It takes: It + takes/took + someone + time + to do st = S + spend/spent + time + V-ing
  • S + find + ít + adj + to do st
  • Prefer: to prefer + N to N / to prefer + Ving to Ving
  • Would rather: would prefer to V + rather than + V = would rather + V + than + V
  • Used to: to be/get used to + V-ing (quen làm gi) vs used to + V (thường làm gi trong quá khứ)
  • Suggestions: Let's/Shall we/Why don't we + V = What about/How about + Ving
  • So that: S + V + so that + S + V = S + V + so as to/in order to/to + V
  • Both...and / Not only...but also / Neither...nor / Either...or
  • Agreement: S + tro động từ/tobe, too / So + tro động từ/tobe + S (khẳng định) | S + tro động từ/tobe + not, either / Neither + tro động từ/tobe + S (phủ định)
  • Suggest: S + suggest + V-ing / S + suggest that + S + should + V1
  • Past simple vs Present perfect: S + last + V2/ed + time + ago = S + have/has + not + V3/ed + for/since... | It's + time + since + S + last + V2/ed | The last time + S + V2/ed + was + time + ago | This is the first time + S + have/has + V3/ed | S + have/has + never + V3/ed + before
  • When did + S + start/begin + to V/Ving? = How long + have/has + S + V3/ed?

exercises.original: I have never read such a romantic story before.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with the words given
exercises.answer: This is the most romantic story I have ever read.
acceptedAnswers: This is the most romantic story I have ever read.
This is the most romantic story I have ever read
exercises.explanation: Pattern: This is thế + superlative + noun + S + have/has + ever + V3.

exercises.original: I have never been to the ballet before.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with the words given
exercises.answer: It is the first time I have been to the ballet.
acceptedAnswers: It is the first time I have been to the ballet.
It is the first time I have been to the ballet
exercises.explanation: Pattern: It is the first time + S + have/has + V3.

exercises.original: They have been living in Germany for 5 years now.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with the words given
exercises.answer: It is 5 years since they started living in Germany.
acceptedAnswers: It is 5 years since they started living in Germany.
It is 5 years since they started living in Germany
It has been 5 years since they started living in Germany.
It has been 5 years since they started living in Germany
exercises.explanation: Pattern: It is + time + since + S + V2/ed.

exercises.original: I came to live here three months ago.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with the words given
exercises.answer: I've been living here for three months.
acceptedAnswers: I've been living here for three months.
I've been living here for three months
I have been living here for three months.
exercises.explanation: Past simple with 'ago' converts to present perfect with 'for.'

exercises.original: The Browns haven't visited Paris since 1998.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with the words given
exercises.answer: The last time the Browns visited Paris was in 1998.
acceptedAnswers: The last time the Browns visited Paris was in 1998.
The last time the Browns visited Paris was in 1998
exercises.explanation: Pattern: The last time + S + V2/ed + was + time.

exercises.original: How long have Catherine and Henry been engaged?
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with the words given
exercises.answer: When did Catherine and Henry get engaged?
acceptedAnswers: When did Catherine and Henry get engaged?
When did Catherine and Henry get engaged
exercises.explanation: How long + have/has + V3? = When did + S + V?

exercises.original: I had a cat once, but now I don't any more.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with the words given
exercises.answer: I used to have a cat.
acceptedAnswers: I used to have a cat.
I used to have a cat
exercises.explanation: 'Used to + V' expresses a past habit or state that no longer exists.

exercises.original: When I was a child, I usually went to the local park every Sunday afternoon.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with the words given
exercises.starterText: When I was a child, I used
exercises.answer: When I was a child, I used to go to the local park every Sunday afternoon.
acceptedAnswers: When I was a child, I used to go to the local park every Sunday afternoon.
When I was a child, I used to go to the local park every Sunday afternoon
exercises.explanation: 'Used to + V' replaces 'usually + V2/ed' for past habits.

exercises.original: Farmers grow rice in this field.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with the words given
exercises.answer: Rice is grown in this field by farmers.
acceptedAnswers: Rice is grown in this field by farmers.
Rice is grown in this field.
Rice is grown in this field by farmers
exercises.explanation: Active to passive: S + be + V3 + by + agent.

exercises.original: He is carrying out research on how people spend their money on clothes.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with the words given
exercises.answer: Research is being carried out on how people spend their money on clothes.
acceptedAnswers: Research is being carried out on how people spend their money on clothes.
Research is being carried out on how people spend their money on clothes
exercises.explanation: Present continuous passive: S + is/are + being + V3.

exercises.original: The police have just released John.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with the words given
exercises.answer: John has just been released by the police.
acceptedAnswers: John has just been released by the police.
John has just been released by the police
John has just been released.
John has just been released
exercises.explanation: Present perfect passive: S + have/has + been + V3.

exercises.original: Jack's parents made him study a lot for the entrance exam to high school.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with the words given
exercises.answer: Jack was made to study a lot for the entrance exam to high school by his parents.
acceptedAnswers: Jack was made to study a lot for the entrance exam to high school by his parents.
Jack was made to study a lot for the entrance exam to high school.
exercises.explanation: Passive of 'make someone do': S + was/were + made + to V.

exercises.original: You must see the manager tomorrow morning.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with the words given
exercises.answer: The manager must be seen tomorrow morning.
acceptedAnswers: The manager must be seen tomorrow morning.
The manager must be seen by you tomorrow morning.
The manager must be seen tomorrow morning
The manager must be seen by you tomorrow morning
exercises.explanation: Modal passive: S + modal + be + V3.

exercises.original: The mechanic serviced my car last week.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with the words given
exercises.answer: I had my car serviced last week.
acceptedAnswers: I had my car serviced last week.
I had my car serviced last week
exercises.explanation: Causative: have + object + V3 (have something done).

exercises.original: People expect that he will join the company soon.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with the words given
exercises.answer: He is expected to join the company soon.
acceptedAnswers: He is expected to join the company soon.
He is expected to join the company soon
exercises.explanation: Pattern: S + is/are + expected + to V.

exercises.original: They believe that touching the head of a Buddha statue is a sign of disrespect.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with the words given
exercises.answer: It is believed that touching the head of a Buddha statue is a sign of disrespect.
acceptedAnswers: It is believed that touching the head of a Buddha statue is a sign of disrespect.
It is believed that touching the head of a Buddha statue is a sign of disrespect
exercises.explanation: Pattern: It + is + believed + that + clause.

exercises.original: The leak was repaired by the plumber this morning.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with the words given
exercises.answer: The plumber repaired the leak this morning.
acceptedAnswers: The plumber repaired the leak this morning.
The plumber repaired the leak this morning
exercises.explanation: Passive to active: the agent becomes the subject.

exercises.original: She knows more about ít than I do.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with the words given
exercises.answer: I don't know as much about ít as she does.
acceptedAnswers: I don't know as much about ít as she does.
I don't know as much about ít as she does
I do not know as much about ít as she does.
exercises.explanation: Comparative to negative equality: not + as...as.

exercises.original: Apples are usually cheaper than oranges.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with the words given
exercises.answer: Apples are not usually as expensive as oranges.
acceptedAnswers: Apples are not usually as expensive as oranges.
Apples are not usually as expensive as oranges
exercises.explanation: Cheaper than = not as expensive as (using the opposite adjective).

exercises.original: Sally is the cleverest student in the class.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with the words given
exercises.answer: Nobody in the class is as clever as Sally.
acceptedAnswers: Nobody in the class is as clever as Sally.
Nobody in the class is cleverer than Sally.
exercises.explanation: Superlative = Nobody + as...as / Nobody + comparative + than.

exercises.original: No one in this club is a better swimmer than John.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with the words given
exercises.answer: John swims the best in this club.
acceptedAnswers: John swims the best in this club.
John swims better than anyone else in this club.
exercises.explanation: No one...better than = S + V + the best.

exercises.original: As I get older, I want to travel less.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with the words given
exercises.answer: The older I get, the less I want to travel.
acceptedAnswers: The older I get, the less I want to travel.
The older I get, the less I want to travel
exercises.explanation: Double comparative: The + comparative + S + V, thế + comparative + S + V.

exercises.original: Although he had a good salary, he was unhappy in his job.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with the words given
exercises.answer: In spite of having a good salary, he was unhappy in his job.
acceptedAnswers: In spite of having a good salary, he was unhappy in his job.
In spite of his good salary, he was unhappy in his job.
exercises.explanation: Although + clause = In spite of + V-ing/noun phrase.

exercises.original: In spite of the cold weather, we went out.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with the words given
exercises.answer: Although the weather was cold, we went out.
acceptedAnswers: Although the weather was cold, we went out.
Although it was cold, we went out.
exercises.explanation: In spite of + noun phrase = Although + clause.

exercises.original: She was seriously ill but she enjoyed life very much.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with the words given
exercises.answer: Although she was seriously ill, she enjoyed life very much.
acceptedAnswers: Although she was seriously ill, she enjoyed life very much.
Although she was seriously ill, she enjoyed life very much
exercises.explanation: Use 'although' to join two contrasting clauses.

exercises.original: Hoa was busy, so she couldn't go to the party.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with the words given
exercises.answer: Because Hoa was busy, she couldn't go to the party.
acceptedAnswers: Because Hoa was busy, she couldn't go to the party.
Because Hoa was busy, she couldn't go to the party
Because Hoa was busy, she could not go to the party.
exercises.explanation: 'So' (result) converts to 'because' (reason) by rearranging the clauses.

exercises.original: Ba was lazy, so he failed the final exam.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with the words given
exercises.answer: Because of his laziness, Ba failed the final exam.
acceptedAnswers: Because of his laziness, Ba failed the final exam.
Because of being lazy, Ba failed the final exam.
exercises.explanation: Because of + noun/gerund phrase (not a clause).

exercises.original: She said, "I ấm taking an important exam next week".
exercises.instruction: Rewrite in reported speech
exercises.answer: She said she was taking an important exam the following week.
acceptedAnswers: She said she was taking an important exam the following week.
She said that she was taking an important exam the following week.
exercises.explanation: Reported speech: ấm -> was, next week -> the following week.

exercises.original: "Where are you going, Peter?", said Susan.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite in reported speech
exercises.answer: Susan asked Peter where he was going.
acceptedAnswers: Susan asked Peter where he was going.
Susan asked Peter where he was going
exercises.explanation: Reported question: question word + S + V (statement order), tense shift.

exercises.original: "Please do not litter as you are walking along the coast", the guard said to us.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite in reported speech
exercises.answer: The guard told us not to litter as we were walking along the coast.
acceptedAnswers: The guard told us not to litter as we were walking along the coast.
The guard told us not to litter as we were walking along the coast
exercises.explanation: Reported request: told + object + (not) to V.

exercises.original: "Why don't we visit the Royal Citadel first?", Thanh said.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite in reported speech
exercises.answer: Thanh suggested that they should visit the Royal Citadel first.
acceptedAnswers: Thanh suggested that they should visit the Royal Citadel first.
Thanh suggested that they visit the Royal Citadel first.
exercises.explanation: Reported suggestion: S + suggested that + S + (should) + V.

exercises.original: "You'd better spend more time learning English, Mary." said the mom.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite in reported speech
exercises.answer: Mary's mom advised her to spend more time learning English.
acceptedAnswers: Mary's mom advised her to spend more time learning English.
Mary's mom advised Mary to spend more time learning English.
exercises.explanation: Reported advice with 'had better': S + advised + object + to V.

exercises.original: She doesn't have a computer or a smartphone, so she can't study online.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using conditional
exercises.answer: If she had a computer or a smartphone, she could study online.
acceptedAnswers: If she had a computer or a smartphone, she could study online.
If she had a computer or a smartphone, she could study online
If she had a computer or a smartphone, she would be able to study online.
exercises.explanation: Second conditional: If + S + V2/ed, S + would/could + V.

exercises.original: You can't register for a course abroad if you don't have a passport.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with the words given
exercises.answer: Unless you have a passport, you can't register for a course abroad.
acceptedAnswers: Unless you have a passport, you can't register for a course abroad.
Unless you have a passport, you can't register for a course abroad
Unless you have a passport, you cannot register for a course abroad.
Unless you have a passport, you cannot register for a course abroad
exercises.explanation: If...not = Unless (remove the negative).

exercises.original: They work slowly and ineffectively.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with the words given
exercises.answer: I wish they worked faster and more effectively.
acceptedAnswers: I wish they worked faster and more effectively.
I wish they didn't work slowly and ineffectively.
I wish they worked faster and more effectively
I wish they would work faster and more effectively.
exercises.explanation: Wish + past simple for present unreal wishes.

exercises.original: I will be late for school.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with the words given
exercises.answer: I wish I weren't late for school.
acceptedAnswers: I wish I weren't late for school.
I wish I wouldn't be late for school.
I wish I weren't going to be late for school.
I wish I wasn't late for school.
I wish I wasn't going to be late for school.
exercises.explanation: Wish + past simple for unreal present/future wishes.

exercises.original: I'm sorry I missed your birthday party.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with the words given
exercises.answer: I wish I hadn't missed your birthday party.
acceptedAnswers: I wish I hadn't missed your birthday party.
I wish I hadn't missed your birthday party
I wish I had not missed your birthday party.
I wish I had not missed your birthday party
exercises.explanation: Wish + past perfect for past regrets.

exercises.original: It's a pity I didn't take my doctor's advice.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with the words given
exercises.answer: I wish I had taken my doctor's advice.
acceptedAnswers: I wish I had taken my doctor's advice.
I wish I had taken my doctor's advice
exercises.explanation: Wish + past perfect for past regrets.

exercises.original: He prefers playing computer games to watching TV.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with the words given
exercises.answer: He'd rather play computer games than watch TV.
acceptedAnswers: He'd rather play computer games than watch TV.
He'd rather play computer games than watch TV
exercises.explanation: Would rather + V + than + V.

exercises.original: I would like you to help me put the chair away.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with the words given
exercises.answer: Do you mind helping me put the chair away?
acceptedAnswers: Do you mind helping me put the chair away?
Do you mind helping me put the chair away
exercises.explanation: Do you mind + V-ing = Would you like to help.

exercises.original: I'm always nervous when I travel by air.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with the words given
exercises.answer: Traveling by air always makes me nervous.
acceptedAnswers: Traveling by air always makes me nervous.
Travelling by air always makes me nervous.
exercises.explanation: Gerund as subject + makes + object + adjective.

exercises.original: He suggested visiting Moc Chau to learn about Thai and H'mong people.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with the words given
exercises.answer: "What about visiting Moc Chau to learn about Thai and H'mong people?"
acceptedAnswers: "What about visiting Moc Chau to learn about Thai and H'mong people?"
What about visiting Moc Chau to learn about Thai and H'mong people?
exercises.explanation: Suggest + V-ing = What about + V-ing?

exercises.original: Keeping the environment clean is very important.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with the words given
exercises.answer: It's very important to keep the environment clean.
acceptedAnswers: It's very important to keep the environment clean.
It's very important to keep the environment clean
It is very important to keep the environment clean.
exercises.explanation: Gerund subject -> It + be + adj + to V.

exercises.original: It is necessary to finish the work today.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with the words given
exercises.answer: You need to finish the work today.
acceptedAnswers: You need to finish the work today.
You need to finish the work today
exercises.explanation: It is necessary to V = You need to V.

exercises.original: Sally finally managed to get the job.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with the words given
exercises.answer: Sally finally succeeded in getting the job.
acceptedAnswers: Sally finally succeeded in getting the job.
Sally finally succeeded in getting the job
exercises.explanation: Manage to V = succeed in V-ing.

exercises.original: I walk to school in 20 minutes everyday.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with the words given
exercises.answer: It takes me 20 minutes to walk to school everyday.
acceptedAnswers: It takes me 20 minutes to walk to school everyday.
It takes me 20 minutes to walk to school every day.
exercises.explanation: It takes + someone + time + to V.

exercises.original: We often spend 30 minutes reviewing our lesson before class.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with the words given
exercises.answer: It often takes us 30 minutes to review our lesson before class.
acceptedAnswers: It often takes us 30 minutes to review our lesson before class.
It often takes us 30 minutes to review our lesson before class
exercises.explanation: S + spend + time + V-ing = It takes + S + time + to V.

exercises.original: It took him twelve hours to fly from Hanoi to London.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with the words given
exercises.answer: He spent twelve hours flying from Hanoi to London.
acceptedAnswers: He spent twelve hours flying from Hanoi to London.
He spent twelve hours flying from Hanoi to London
exercises.explanation: It takes + S + time + to V = S + spend + time + V-ing.

exercises.original: Tam doesn't type as fast as she used to.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with the words given
exercises.answer: Tam used to type faster than she does now.
acceptedAnswers: Tam used to type faster than she does now.
Tam used to type faster.
exercises.explanation: Not as...as now = used to + comparative.

exercises.original: I'm really excited about the upcoming pottery workshop.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with the words given
exercises.answer: I'm looking forward to the upcoming pottery workshop.
acceptedAnswers: I'm looking forward to the upcoming pottery workshop.
I'm looking forward to the upcoming pottery workshop
exercises.explanation: Be excited about = look forward to + noun/V-ing.

exercises.original: I last read a book on September 20th.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (since)
exercises.answer: I haven't read a book since September 20th.
acceptedAnswers: I haven't read a book since September 20th.
I haven't read a book since September 20th
I have not read a book since September 20th.
I have not read a book since September 20th
exercises.explanation: S + last + V2/ed = S + haven't/hasn't + V3 + since + time.

exercises.original: Ann began to be interested in healthy eating 3 years ago.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (has)
exercises.answer: Ann has been interested in healthy eating for 3 years.
acceptedAnswers: Ann has been interested in healthy eating for 3 years.
Ann has been interested in healthy eating for 3 years
exercises.explanation: Began + time + ago = has/have + V3 + for + time.

exercises.original: Someone is going to redecorate the kitchen for us next month.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (have)
exercises.answer: We are going to have the kitchen redecorated next month.
acceptedAnswers: We are going to have the kitchen redecorated next month.
We are going to have the kitchen redecorated next month
exercises.explanation: Causative: have + something + V3 (have something done).

exercises.original: People hope that cable-car construction to Son Doong Cave will be stopped.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (is)
exercises.answer: It is hoped that cable-car construction to Son Doong Cave will be stopped.
acceptedAnswers: It is hoped that cable-car construction to Son Doong Cave will be stopped.
It is hoped that cable-car construction to Son Doong Cave will be stopped
exercises.explanation: People hope that... = It is hoped that...

exercises.original: "When are you going to submit your research paper?", my teacher asked me.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (was)
exercises.answer: My teacher asked me when I was going to submit my research paper.
acceptedAnswers: My teacher asked me when I was going to submit my research paper.
My teacher asked me when I was going to submit my research paper
exercises.explanation: Reported question with tense shift: are going -> was going.

exercises.original: Mr. Son spends all his life saving patients. We all admire him.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (who)
exercises.answer: Mr. Son, who spends all his life saving patients, is admired by us all.
acceptedAnswers: Mr. Son, who spends all his life saving patients, is admired by us all.
Mr. Son, who we all admire, spends all his life saving patients.
Mr. Son, whom we all admire, spends all his life saving patients.
exercises.explanation: Relative clause with 'who' for non-defining clause.

exercises.original: Andy has a lovely cat. Its fur is white.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (whose)
exercises.answer: Andy has a lovely cat whose fur is white.
acceptedAnswers: Andy has a lovely cat whose fur is white.
Andy has a lovely cat whose fur is white
exercises.explanation: Relative pronoun 'whose' shows possession.

exercises.original: Mr. Dean's team has lost the game. He looks very sad.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (whose)
exercises.answer: Mr Dean, whose team has lost the game, looks very sad.
acceptedAnswers: Mr Dean, whose team has lost the game, looks very sad.
Mr. Dean, whose team has lost the game, looks very sad.
exercises.explanation: Non-defining relative clause with 'whose' for possession.

exercises.original: I don't have any facebook account. I want to have one soon.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (wish)
exercises.answer: I wish I had a facebook account.
acceptedAnswers: I wish I had a facebook account.
I wish I had a facebook account
exercises.explanation: Wish + past simple for present unreal wishes.

exercises.original: It's pity she doesn't share memories of her trip with me.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (wish)
exercises.answer: I wish she shared memories of her trip with me.
acceptedAnswers: I wish she shared memories of her trip with me.
I wish she would share memories of her trip with me.
exercises.explanation: Wish + past simple for present unreal situations.

exercises.original: It's a pity. I didn't study harder for the exam.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (wish)
exercises.answer: I wish I had studied harder for the exam.
acceptedAnswers: I wish I had studied harder for the exam.
I wish I had studied harder for the exam
exercises.explanation: Wish + past perfect for past regrets.

exercises.original: "Shall we surf the net for the location of Ninh Binh Province?", said she.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (suggested)
exercises.answer: She suggested surfing the net for the location of Ninh Binh Province.
acceptedAnswers: She suggested surfing the net for the location of Ninh Binh Province.
She suggested that they should surf the net for the location of Ninh Binh Province.
She suggested that they surf the net for the location of Ninh Binh Province.
exercises.explanation: Shall we...? = S + suggested + V-ing.

exercises.original: This book is very boring and so is that one.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (as)
exercises.answer: This book is as boring as that one.
acceptedAnswers: This book is as boring as that one.
This book is as boring as that one
exercises.explanation: Both equally boring = as...as comparison.

exercises.original: She visits more countries. She has more friends.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (more)
exercises.answer: The more countries she visits, the more friends she has.
acceptedAnswers: The more countries she visits, the more friends she has.
The more countries she visits, the more friends she has
exercises.explanation: Double comparative: The more...the more...

exercises.original: I don't know much about historical events, so I can't help her with her history homework.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (if)
exercises.answer: I could help her with her history homework if I knew more about historical events.
acceptedAnswers: I could help her with her history homework if I knew more about historical events.
If I knew more about historical events, I could help her with her history homework.
exercises.explanation: Second conditional: If + V2/ed, S + would/could + V.

exercises.original: I may not have to work tonight. I will go out for dinner.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (if)
exercises.answer: I will go out for dinner if I don't have to work tonight.
acceptedAnswers: I will go out for dinner if I don't have to work tonight.
If I don't have to work tonight, I will go out for dinner.
I will go out for dinner if I do not have to work tonight.
If I do not have to work tonight, I will go out for dinner.
exercises.explanation: First conditional: If + present simple, S + will + V.

exercises.original: I didn't take my parents' advice, so I got into a lot of troubles.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (if)
exercises.answer: If I had taken my parents' advice, I wouldn't have got into a lot of troubles.
acceptedAnswers: If I had taken my parents' advice, I wouldn't have got into a lot of troubles.
If I had taken my parents' advice, I wouldn't have gotten into a lot of troubles.
If I had taken my parents' advice, I would not have got into a lot of troubles.
If I had taken my parents' advice, I wouldn't have got into a lot of trouble.
If I had taken my parents' advice, I would not have gotten into a lot of troubles.
If I had taken my parents' advice, I would not have got into a lot of trouble.
exercises.explanation: Third conditional: If + had + V3, S + would have + V3.

exercises.original: I'm interested in the news about ancient towns.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (find)
exercises.answer: I find the news about ancient towns interesting.
acceptedAnswers: I find the news about ancient towns interesting.
I find the news about ancient towns interesting
exercises.explanation: Be interested in = find + something + interesting.

exercises.original: It is going to rain. You should bring a raincoat with you.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (so)
exercises.answer: It is going to rain, so you should bring a raincoat with you.
acceptedAnswers: It is going to rain, so you should bring a raincoat with you.
It is going to rain, so you should bring a raincoat with you
exercises.explanation: 'So' connects a cause to its result.

exercises.original: I couldn't go to the airport to see her off because I was ill.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (because of)
exercises.answer: I couldn't go to the airport to see her off because of my illness.
acceptedAnswers: I couldn't go to the airport to see her off because of my illness.
I couldn't go to the airport to see her off because of being ill.
I could not go to the airport to see her off because of my illness.
exercises.explanation: Because + clause = Because of + noun/gerund.

exercises.original: How long have you had your new car?
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (get)
exercises.answer: When did you get your new car?
acceptedAnswers: When did you get your new car?
When did you get your new car
exercises.explanation: How long have you had = When did you get.

exercises.original: Leave the shop or I will call the police.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (unless)
exercises.answer: I'll call the police unless you leave the shop.
acceptedAnswers: I'll call the police unless you leave the shop.
I'll call the police unless you leave the shop
exercises.explanation: Or = unless (if...not).

exercises.original: The Lord of Brecon built this castle in 1286.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (by)
exercises.answer: This castle was built by the Lord of Brecon in 1286.
acceptedAnswers: This castle was built by the Lord of Brecon in 1286.
This castle was built in 1286 by the Lord of Brecon.
exercises.explanation: Active to passive: S + was/were + V3 + by + agent.

exercises.original: Our teachers never let us leave the school.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (allowed)
exercises.answer: We were not allowed to leave the school.
acceptedAnswers: We were not allowed to leave the school.
We were not allowed to leave the school by our teachers.
exercises.explanation: Never let = were not allowed to.

exercises.original: During the film on TV, the phone rang.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (was)
exercises.answer: While I was watching the film on TV, the phone rang.
acceptedAnswers: While I was watching the film on TV, the phone rang.
While I was watching the film on TV, the phone rang
exercises.explanation: During + noun = While + S + was/were + V-ing.

exercises.original: Do you have any plans for next Wednesday evening?
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (doing)
exercises.answer: What are you doing next Wednesday evening?
acceptedAnswers: What are you doing next Wednesday evening?
What are you doing next Wednesday evening
exercises.explanation: Have plans for = What are you doing (present continuous for future plans).

exercises.original: The strong winds blew down the two ancient trees during the storm.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (blown)
exercises.answer: The two ancient trees were blown down by the strong winds during the storm.
acceptedAnswers: The two ancient trees were blown down by the strong winds during the storm.
The two ancient trees were blown down during the storm.
exercises.explanation: Passive: S + were + V3 (blown) + by agent.

exercises.original: He doesn't have enough money to buy the computer.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (too)
exercises.answer: The computer is too expensive for him to buy.
acceptedAnswers: The computer is too expensive for him to buy.
The computer is too expensive for him to buy
exercises.explanation: Not enough = too + opposite adjective + for sb + to V.

exercises.original: "You should start a new hobby, Mr. Smith" the doctor said.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (take)
exercises.answer: The doctor advised Mr. Smith to take up a new hobby.
acceptedAnswers: The doctor advised Mr. Smith to take up a new hobby.
The doctor advised Mr. Smith to take up a new hobby
exercises.explanation: Reported advice: advised + object + to V. 'Start' = 'take up' a hobby.

exercises.original: The man suddenly realized that the neighbor was watching him.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (watched)
exercises.starterText: The man suddenly realized that he
exercises.answer: The man suddenly realized that he was being watched by the neighbor.
acceptedAnswers: The man suddenly realized that he was being watched by the neighbor.
The man suddenly realized that he was being watched by the neighbour.
exercises.explanation: Past continuous passive: was/were + being + V3.

exercises.original: I could never have passed the exam without your help.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (you)
exercises.answer: I could never have passed the exam if you hadn't helped me.
acceptedAnswers: I could never have passed the exam if you hadn't helped me.
I could never have passed the exam if you had not helped me.
I could never have passed the exam if you hadn't helped me
I could never have passed the exam if you had not helped me
exercises.explanation: Without your help = if you hadn't helped me (third conditional).

exercises.original: We paid some people to landscape the garden for us last year.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (had)
exercises.answer: We had the garden landscaped last year.
acceptedAnswers: We had the garden landscaped last year.
We had the garden landscaped for us last year.
exercises.explanation: Causative: have + something + V3.

exercises.original: "How about going to the cinema?", said Kate.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (suggested)
exercises.answer: Kate suggested going to the cinema.
acceptedAnswers: Kate suggested going to the cinema.
Kate suggested that they go to the cinema.
Kate suggested that they should go to the cinema.
exercises.explanation: How about + V-ing = S + suggested + V-ing.

exercises.original: My advice to you is to talk to your parents.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (would)
exercises.answer: If I were you, I would talk to my parents.
acceptedAnswers: If I were you, I would talk to my parents.
If I were you, I would talk to your parents.
If I were you, I'd talk to my parents.
If I was you, I would talk to my parents.
exercises.explanation: My advice = If I were you, I would...

exercises.original: Mary wasn't strong enough to lift the box.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (weak)
exercises.answer: Mary was too weak to lift the box.
acceptedAnswers: Mary was too weak to lift the box.
Mary was too weak to lift the box
exercises.explanation: Not strong enough = too weak to V.

exercises.original: They wanted to apologize for their behavior: that's why they paid for dinner.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (in order)
exercises.answer: They paid for dinner in order to apologize for their behavior.
acceptedAnswers: They paid for dinner in order to apologize for their behavior.
They paid for dinner in order to apologise for their behaviour.
exercises.explanation: That's why = in order to (purpose).

exercises.original: It isn't necessary to bring drinks as they are included in the package.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (have to)
exercises.answer: You don't have to bring drinks as they are included in the package.
acceptedAnswers: You don't have to bring drinks as they are included in the package.
You don't have to bring drinks as they are included in the package
You do not have to bring drinks as they are included in the package.
exercises.explanation: It isn't necessary = don't have to.

exercises.original: Tom said, "I will be playing tennis when you arrive."
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (He)
exercises.answer: Tom said he would be playing tennis when I arrived.
acceptedAnswers: Tom said he would be playing tennis when I arrived.
Tom said that he would be playing tennis when I arrived.
exercises.explanation: Reported speech: will -> would, you -> I, arrive -> arrived.

exercises.original: When I was a child, I would go to the park every Saturday afternoon.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (used)
exercises.answer: When I was a child, I used to go to the park every Saturday afternoon.
acceptedAnswers: When I was a child, I used to go to the park every Saturday afternoon.
When I was a child, I used to go to the park every Saturday afternoon
exercises.explanation: Would + V (past habit) = used to + V.

exercises.original: They moved to this suburb in 1990.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (lived)
exercises.answer: They have lived in this suburb since 1990.
acceptedAnswers: They have lived in this suburb since 1990.
They have lived in this suburb since 1990
exercises.explanation: Past simple + time = present perfect + since + time.

exercises.original: Mike was interested in North American history in his school days.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (interest)
exercises.answer: Mike took an interest in North American history in his school days.
acceptedAnswers: Mike took an interest in North American history in his school days.
Mike had an interest in North American history in his school days.
exercises.explanation: Was interested in = took/had an interest in.

exercises.original: When ít comes to population, Beijing is much bigger than Auckland's.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (populated)
exercises.answer: Beijing is much more populated than Auckland.
acceptedAnswers: Beijing is much more populated than Auckland.
Beijing is much more heavily populated than Auckland.
exercises.explanation: Bigger in population = more populated.

exercises.original: Do you think you could give me a hand to move the sofa?
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (mind)
exercises.answer: Would you mind giving me a hand to move the sofa?
acceptedAnswers: Would you mind giving me a hand to move the sofa?
Would you mind giving me a hand to move the sofa
Would you mind helping me move the sofa?
exercises.explanation: Could you = Would you mind + V-ing.

exercises.original: I think Jerry really likes Simon.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (keen)
exercises.answer: Jerry is keen on Simon.
acceptedAnswers: Jerry is keen on Simon.
Jerry is really keen on Simon.
exercises.explanation: Really likes = is keen on.

exercises.original: The Queen is opening the new school.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (opened)
exercises.answer: The new school is being opened by the Queen.
acceptedAnswers: The new school is being opened by the Queen.
The new school is being opened by the Queen
exercises.explanation: Present continuous passive: is/are + being + V3.

exercises.original: Mark started learning French two years ago.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (learned)
exercises.answer: Mark has learned French for two years.
acceptedAnswers: Mark has learned French for two years.
Mark has learnt French for two years.
Mark has been learning French for two years.
exercises.explanation: Started + time + ago = has + V3 + for + time.

exercises.original: Do you own that car?
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (yours)
exercises.answer: Is that car yours?
acceptedAnswers: Is that car yours?
Is that car yours
exercises.explanation: Do you own = Is + noun + yours?

exercises.original: Look at those black clouds! There is rain arriving.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (going)
exercises.starterText: Look at those black clouds! It
exercises.answer: Look at those black clouds! It is going to rain.
acceptedAnswers: Look at those black clouds! It is going to rain.
Look at those black clouds! It is going to rain
Look at those black clouds! It's going to rain.
Look at those black clouds! It's going to rain
exercises.explanation: There is rain arriving = It is going to rain.

exercises.original: I don't have a new computer.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (wish)
exercises.answer: I wish I had a new computer.
acceptedAnswers: I wish I had a new computer.
I wish I had a new computer
exercises.explanation: Wish + past simple for present unreal wishes.

exercises.original: The teenagers were stealing my car when I arrived.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (being)
exercises.answer: My car was being stolen by the teenagers when I arrived.
acceptedAnswers: My car was being stolen by the teenagers when I arrived.
My car was being stolen when I arrived.
exercises.explanation: Past continuous passive: was/were + being + V3.

exercises.original: The last time John saw me was in 2000.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (since)
exercises.answer: John hasn't seen me since 2000.
acceptedAnswers: John hasn't seen me since 2000.
John hasn't seen me since 2000
John has not seen me since 2000.
John has not seen me since 2000
exercises.explanation: The last time + V2/ed + was + time = haven't/hasn't + V3 + since + time.

exercises.original: I haven't gone swimming for nearly a year.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (last)
exercises.answer: The last time I went swimming was nearly a year ago.
acceptedAnswers: The last time I went swimming was nearly a year ago.
The last time I went swimming was nearly a year ago
exercises.explanation: Haven't + V3 + for = The last time + V2/ed + was + time + ago.

exercises.original: I regret not speaking to Ms. Hoa sooner.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (had)
exercises.answer: I wish I had spoken to Ms. Hoa sooner.
acceptedAnswers: I wish I had spoken to Ms. Hoa sooner.
I wish I had spoken to Ms. Hoa sooner
exercises.explanation: Regret not + V-ing = wish + had + V3 (past regret).

exercises.original: Yesterday, they cut down two old trees in the local park.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (were)
exercises.answer: Yesterday, two old trees in the local park were cut down.
acceptedAnswers: Yesterday, two old trees in the local park were cut down.
Yesterday, two old trees were cut down in the local park.
exercises.explanation: Active to passive with past simple.

exercises.original: I walked quietly because I didn't want to wake the baby.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (so as)
exercises.answer: I walked quietly so as not to wake the baby.
acceptedAnswers: I walked quietly so as not to wake the baby.
I walked quietly so as not to wake the baby
exercises.explanation: Because + didn't want = so as not to V (purpose).

exercises.original: This is the first time I have ever drunk coffee.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (never)
exercises.answer: I have never drunk coffee before.
acceptedAnswers: I have never drunk coffee before.
I have never drunk coffee before
I've never drunk coffee before.
I've never drunk coffee before
exercises.explanation: This is the first time + have V3 = have never + V3 + before.

exercises.original: Do you know how many letters are delivered by the post office every year?
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (delivers)
exercises.answer: Do you know how many letters the post office delivers every year?
acceptedAnswers: Do you know how many letters the post office delivers every year?
Do you know how many letters the post office delivers every year
exercises.explanation: Passive to active: move agent to subject position.

exercises.original: Stop interrupting me all the time?
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (would)
exercises.answer: I wish you would stop interrupting me all the time.
acceptedAnswers: I wish you would stop interrupting me all the time.
I wish you would stop interrupting me all the time
exercises.explanation: Wish + would for complaints about annoying behavior.

exercises.original: My car keys are possibly in the kitchen.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (might)
exercises.answer: My car keys might be in the kitchen.
acceptedAnswers: My car keys might be in the kitchen.
My car keys might be in the kitchen
exercises.explanation: Possibly = might (expressing possibility).

exercises.original: Is ít a problem if I open the window a little?
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (mind)
exercises.answer: Would you mind if I opened the window a little?
acceptedAnswers: Would you mind if I opened the window a little?
Would you mind if I opened the window a little
exercises.explanation: Is ít a problem if = Would you mind if + past simple.

exercises.original: Kate is a teacher.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (as)
exercises.answer: Kate works as a teacher.
acceptedAnswers: Kate works as a teacher.
Kate works as a teacher
exercises.explanation: Is a teacher = works as a teacher.

exercises.original: I met your sister by chance at the airport last week.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (ran)
exercises.answer: I ran into your sister at the airport last week.
acceptedAnswers: I ran into your sister at the airport last week.
I ran into your sister at the airport last week
exercises.explanation: Met by chance = ran into (phrasal verb).

exercises.original: The doctor told me to give up smoking.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (stop)
exercises.answer: The doctor told me to stop smoking.
acceptedAnswers: The doctor told me to stop smoking.
The doctor told me to stop smoking
exercises.explanation: Give up = stop.

exercises.original: Why don't we leave after breakfast on Saturday?
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (leaving)
exercises.answer: How about leaving after breakfast on Saturday?
acceptedAnswers: How about leaving after breakfast on Saturday?
How about leaving after breakfast on Saturday
exercises.explanation: Why don't we + V = How about + V-ing?

exercises.original: This is the most boring film I have ever seen!
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (more)
exercises.answer: I have never seen a more boring film than this!
acceptedAnswers: I have never seen a more boring film than this!
I have never seen a more boring film than this
exercises.explanation: The most + adj + ever = never + a more + adj + than this.

exercises.original: We will have to leave early tomorrow.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (set)
exercises.answer: We will have to set off early tomorrow.
acceptedAnswers: We will have to set off early tomorrow.
We will have to set out early tomorrow.
exercises.explanation: Leave = set off/set out (phrasal verb for starting a journey).

exercises.original: We should leave here now.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (left)
exercises.answer: It's time we left here.
acceptedAnswers: It's time we left here.
It's time we left here now.
It is time we left here.
It is time we left here now.
exercises.explanation: Should V now = It's time + S + V2/ed.

exercises.original: He locked the door to prevent the prisoner escaping.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (so that)
exercises.answer: He locked the door so that the prisoner couldn't escape.
acceptedAnswers: He locked the door so that the prisoner couldn't escape.
He locked the door so that the prisoner would not escape.
He locked the door so that the prisoner could not escape.
He locked the door so that the prisoner wouldn't escape.
exercises.explanation: To prevent = so that + S + couldn't/wouldn't.

exercises.original: Please don't smoke in this room.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (rather)
exercises.answer: I'd rather you didn't smoke in this room.
acceptedAnswers: I'd rather you didn't smoke in this room.
I'd rather you didn't smoke in this room
I'd rather you did not smoke in this room.
I would rather you didn't smoke in this room.
I would rather you did not smoke in this room.
exercises.explanation: Would rather + S + V2/ed (past subjunctive for polite requests).

exercises.original: The weather was beautiful but we didn't go on the picnic.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite using the word in brackets: (in spite)
exercises.answer: We didn't go on the picnic in spite of the beautiful weather.
acceptedAnswers: We didn't go on the picnic in spite of the beautiful weather.
We didn't go on the picnic in spite of the beautiful weather
We did not go on the picnic in spite of the beautiful weather.
exercises.explanation: But = in spite of (showing contrast).
